item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: …Okay I have no idea what this was. Kind of boring and have no idea what the big deal is or why these 3 separate scps are connected.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love this, especially how the "safe" class anomaly is actually YASH, the most dangerous of the three profanities, +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Cant believe I didn't upvote this already.
Superb imagery throughout and a great, sinking feeling towards the reveal of YASH.

Although the "Corpse-Father" from 4935 is heavily implied to be 4812-K, I wonder if it is really the amalgamation of all the great profanities, finally unleashed to hunt the remnants of humanity at the twilight of their existence.

+1

item.title: Re: Of Three Profanities
item.content:encoded:
Noting that the images used in this article comes from the following sources:

  • Air Force, Public Domain
  • Wikimedia Commons, CC BY-SA 4.0
  • Wikimedia Commons, CC BY-SA 4.0
  • Public Domain Pictures, Public Domain
  • Wikimedia Commons, CC BY-SA 2.0 FR
  • Wikimedia Commons, CC BY-SA 3.0

All of these images are cc-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sir, I would be glad if you write the credits of the pictures so that everyone can translate in ease.
The three musketeers finally got the big boy number

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
THIS IS AWESOME!

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
though spectral analysis has not been possible due to the nature of SCP-4812-S’s anomalous makeup.

Blind personnel could take a sample an feed it to an optical spectrometer. If the image produced (like shown here) by that caused the same effects, that should be noted in article, as the spectrum of a substance generally isn't considered to be an picture of the substance. And if the visual spectrum causes the anomalous effect, it could be rendered as a line graph (like here) or just a list of numbers. And if even those still caused the anomalous effect, that should also be noted in the article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I just started reading your skips, Kaktus. They’re good. Really good. This one is no exception. I really like dramatic irony you incorporated within the last two addendums, and it was just confusing enough to keep me guessing.

However, it does seem like an article based around world building. Almost too much world building, I would say. Nevertheless, it was still developed in such a way that I could enjoy it.

I sill feel like you could clarify 4812-K’s motives a bit better, I personally don’t see why it was searching for all the other profanities.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Nice world building, especially when adding Bigfoot, the Fair Folk, and the Daevites into the mix. However, I now understand why DrClef replaced the Apollyon class in SCP-2317. It's not special anymore.

How many Fair Folk skips do we have now? Enough for a tag?

item.title: I love your work
item.content:encoded:
Every article you write is so well formatted, it feels like a legit government classified document.

Whilst I do agree that having the stinger come in an email or memo, like your other articles, is a bit of a trope at this point, I think it works here. And as you said, it's world building for a bigger story, one that I'm excited to see.

I do however think that this could of done a little more to catch attention, and not just be world building.

Still going to +1 though, because good lord the style and format of your documents are just way too good.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Checked out the post on the subreddit, and that really helped clear up the ending there. Amazing work, kaktus. Can't wait to see what you'll do with this whole thing!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It was confusing at times and I'm still not exactly sure on the details. It caught my interest so here is my upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Can’t wait to see the rest of this project. Upvoted.

Also…

The star maps vexed me, but tracing them in the sky and following the movement of the star that cannot be seen I caught but for a moment a glimpse of the face of the one that La Hire had called the Profane Adamant.

The bolded text… is that the 4000-Eshu Protocol? Faeries are mentioned at other points in the writings.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is fascinating stuff but… I don't get the final memo. Anyone wanna explain?

EDIT: Never mind. kak put an explanation on the subreddit here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is a rare novote on a kaktus article for me. The worldbuilding is fantastically evocative, but the whole article just seeps of that - pure worldbuilding. I have no doubt at all that this whole thing is gonna pay off in a big way, but til then I'm left not feeling much of anything.

This one could use another proofreading pass or 2, as well. Doesn't yet have that distinctive kaktus polish.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As a standalone article, 4812 gives just enough information to intrigue and confuse me at the same time, but the way in which you confuse me has earned my upvote. I can't wait to see what else you do in this fantasy setting to make things a bit clearer because I'm sure this skip's quality will only be improved with some more of that trademark Kaktus storytelling.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Royal Apollyons (implying a crown) and infinite cold, you say? Interesting...

+ nitpicky crit stuff
– hide block

This formatting will make the bullet point line breaks work better for all devices. The span with margin thing only indents the first line on mobile (and uses a pixel measurement and whatever.)

 * **SCP-4812-S:** SCP-4812-S is contained at its location of discovery. The entrance to the cave containing SCP-4812-S is to be sealed by three maximum-security vault doors. Entrance to SCP-4812-S’s site of containment is forbidden to any individuals without proper credentials. Under no circumstances should SCP-4812-S be exposed to any amount of light. SCP-4812-S is to be interacted with only by members of the fully-blind MTF Vestus-12 "Northern Star". _ _ Under no circumstances should SCP-4812-S be exposed to either SCP-4812-E or SCP-4812-K. * **SCP-4812-E:** SCP-4812-E is contained within a thermally controlled vault at Site-80. Due to the nature of SCP-4812-E’s anomalous capabilities, its containment chamber must be kept at a constant 1,400°C. SCP-4812-E itself is contained within a tank of molten iron. Direct maintenance of SCP-4812-E’s containment chamber must take place outside of the cell due to the intense heat within. Special remote drones equipped with hafnium-carbide exteriors are to be used in maintaining the interior of SCP-4812’s containment cell. A drop in internal temperature of more than 50°C must be considered a breach of containment. _ _ Under no circumstances should SCP-4812-E be exposed to either SCP-4812-S or SCP-4812-K. * **SCP-4812-K:** SCP-4812-K is currently uncontained. Due to the location and nature of SCP-4812-K, it is unlikely that its existence will be exposed, but caution must be taken in ensuring that any information pertaining to the entity is suppressed. Foundation information security assets are to monitor web traffic for any images, accounts, or recordings of SCP-4812-K. Further research into the nature of SCP-4812-K is required to prevent additional exposure should the entity present itself on Earth once again. _ _ Under no circumstances should SCP-4812-K be exposed to SCP-4812-S and SCP-4812-E.

By the authority of the United Nations Security Council and the Global Occult Coalition, you are hereby forbidden from further involvement into any supernatural activity involving three specific paranormal entities:

This contradicts the following line, not long after:

Based on information recovered by our agents, it is likely that you have the PLUTO entity in containment at one of your facilities. You are instructed to immediately make all efforts to destroy this entity beyond its ability to self-repair or regenerate.

Does the GOC want the Foundation to stop all further involvement with Pluto or destroy it? They've ordered the Foundation to do both in the same letter.

EDIT:

You also give two different accounts for the discovery of 4812-K:

The existence of SCP-4812-K was not realized until the discovery of SCP-4812-1, due in part to SCP-4812-K’s near-invisibility6 and its high rate of speed as it travels. SCP-4812-K routinely reaches speeds in excess of 800 km/h.

SCP-4812-K has been known to the Foundation since 1964, when it was confused for an experimental US air vehicle while crossing over the northern USSR.

These aren't entirely irreconcilable, but they do appear to give different accounts of how the Foundation learned of it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This has the same issue as some of your other articles, such as SCP-2846, in that you have to deliver the stinger by means of exposition in the form of a missive or email, but the buildup to the stinger is fantastic. Some other things I really like about the piece are how each sub-entity is denoted by its containment class, and how the most dangerous of the Three Profanities is actually Safe. There's a real sense of gravitas, scale, and danger present throughout the piece - that first picture, with the foundry containment unit, is particularly evocative.

item.title: Of Three Profanities
item.content:encoded:
“She was fury, she was wrath, she was vengeance.”

― Sarah J. Maas, Queen of Shadows

Some worldbuilding for part of a much larger project. This will eventually tie in, but I hope it's enjoyable as it is right now. Thanks to the handful of people I showed this to before popping it off.

Image credits to come.
